text: Nexus is a contextual multi-armed bandit.
Its job is to pick the right message for every user, on every channel,
caption: Pick the right message — for every user, every channel.

text: and to get measurably better with each send.
It starts with the user.
Ten signals — conversion rates, when they engage, how deeply they read, whether they give —
caption: 10 signals: channel rates, timing, depth, giving.

text: become a ten-dimensional feature vector.
caption: → a 10-dimensional feature vector

text: Nearby vectors cluster into personas:
caption: Nearby vectors cluster into personas

text: behavioral archetypes discovered by k-means over cosine distance.
caption: k-means++ over cosine distance

text: Each persona gets its own learning model.
Now the choice. Every message variant is an arm.
caption: Every message variant is an arm.

text: Thompson Sampling models each arm's success rate as a Beta distribution,
caption: Thompson Sampling: each arm is a Beta(α, β)

text: draws a random sample from each one, and sends the highest draw.
caption: Draw a sample from each — send the highest.

text: Strong arms win often, but uncertain arms still earn a shot.
caption: Explore and exploit, in one rule.

text: When the user converts — an open, a reading plan, a donation — that becomes a reward.
caption: Conversion → reward

text: It's normalized, clamped, and folded back in.
caption: r = clamp(raw / 100, −1, 1)

text: Good outcomes raise alpha; misses raise beta.
caption: Wins raise α · misses raise β

text: And old evidence quietly decays, so the model stays current.
caption: α, β ×= 0.99  (decay)

text: Send, observe, update — every hour, across push, email, and in-app.
caption: Send · observe · update — every hour

text: In production, that's a thirty-four percent lift over random.
caption: +34% lift over random

text: That's Nexus.
